qa_pairs.question: What kind of software is categorized as site-specific on Compute Canada clusters, and what is the primary reason for this classification?
qa_pairs.answer: Site-specific software includes packages for chemistry (like ADF, AMS, Amber, Gaussian), physics (DIRAC), biology (Galaxy, GBrowse, TPP), and mathematics (SAS). These packages are installed only at certain sites or separately at each site, primarily due to licensing restrictions on the software.

qa_pairs.question: On which specific clusters is the Gaussian computational chemistry software package available as site-specific software?
qa_pairs.answer: The Gaussian computational chemistry software package is available as site-specific software on both the Nibi and Fir clusters.

qa_pairs.question: If a research group wants to use the Galaxy scientific workflow platform on the Cedar cluster, what steps do they need to take?
qa_pairs.answer: Any group on the Cedar cluster can have one Galaxy instance. To set it up, the group needs to contact technical support, as the instance will be run under a shared account created by the administrators.
